item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The horror of this poor persons physical condition (emphasised by the poem she wrote) combined with the ambiguity of why she is creating this effect really sold me on this skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In your defense, the lost first version I posted had a distasteful interview with the perp that I included because the one person in chat who I'd gotten to look at the draft suggested/liked it. The reason I straight-up deleted that first version wasn't with clearing votes in mind, but because, IIRC, it had been triggery for you (and I think one other person?). And my kneejerk reaction to "oh shit I hurt somebody" was "take it down immediately".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Coming back to this one five years later, I'm pretty shocked at how harsh I was on it. It's a great concept with well thought-out containment procedures and a killer stinger (the photographs!). Maybe it was just lingering distaste over the removed genital-eating bit? At any rate, I'm over it now and ready to upvote. Sorry I got on your case about this one, pig.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Please go right ahead. =)

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Well thats one unsettling article. In the opening post Pig_Catapult mentioned that the tale/poem called Four Tines Blind is about this article, and I see no reason why this article shouldn't link there. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-1359 is a human female of Northern European descent with the birth date ██/██/████. It is 168cm tall, and weighs an average of 62kg. SCP-1359 is missing its right eye, right ear, right foot, and the little, ring, and middle fingers on its left hand. It also has heavy scarring from the removal of strips of skin. Any organism that visually observes SCP-1359 or an accurate pictographic depiction thereof will be overcome with a single-minded determination to gain access to SCP-1359, remove some part of SCP-1359's body, and consume it. Once a portion of SCP-1359 has been successfully removed and fully consumed, the urge to continue consuming parts of SCP-1359 subsides until the eaten portion has fully passed through the digestive system.

To this:

SCP-1359 is a human female of Northern European descent with the birth date ██/██/████. It is 168cm tall, and weighs an average of 62kg. SCP-1359 is missing its right eye, right ear, right foot, and the little, ring, and middle fingers on its left hand. It also has heavy scarring from the removal of strips of skin. Any organism that visually observes SCP-1359 or an accurate pictographic depiction thereof will be overcome with a single-minded determination to gain access to SCP-1359, remove some part of SCP-1359's body, and consume it. Once a portion of SCP-1359 has been successfully removed and fully consumed, the urge to continue consuming parts of SCP-1359 subsides until the eaten portion has fully passed through the digestive system.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They don't exist. Lots of SCPs make reference to documents that don't exist, because what you're reading is supposed to be the tip of a much larger iceberg at any given time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where are the documents? for 1359? they make reference to the 'Documents' where are they? :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edited the containment procedures a bit to hopefully reduce the appearance of "lock porn"/make the actual purpose of that part more clear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love mindscrew SCPs, mostly because I like playing the game of figuring out how to work around their effects. This one was no exception. It was fun to read, and fun to think about. Also, I hated the doctor, so I felt some pleasure at this line:

After review by a Human Resources panel, Dr. B█████ was designated D-██████ under Protocol 12.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whether she was a completely normal woman to start with, or already had something anomalous about her that was then warped by the ritual and/or the resulting psychological trauma into what it is now, is a detail I'm choosing to leave ambiguous. Either way, "the limited imposition of madness upon reality" would certainly be a good way to describe this anomaly's origins.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not joking, these were the exact words I physically said when I got to the part about the parents eating her photographs. Not with any irony, not because of any reference to that movie, nothing. I was just that blown away by this SCP. Holy cow this one is good and will be bothering me for awhile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, so she was a normal woman until this delusional doctor kidnapped her and started eating her flesh in a highly ritualised manner? Did he accidentally (or intentionally) perform some occult ritual that imbued her with anomalous properties so that everyone else would would hunger for her the same way he does? If so that's incredibly disturbing. Makes me wonder how many other SCPs have been created by mentally deranged individuals whose anomalous properties were influenced by their psychoses.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Arrrr!

… Dammit, it should not be this easy to picture her, with a peg foot, brandishing a crutch as she orders someone to walk the plank.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There have been, but I didn't feel the need to include any in the article itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my headcanon? Possible maybe. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does this apply to things like mosquitos?

It applies to anything that has sophisticated enough eyes to see her clearly, so, uh, yes, I think? I'm no expert on the visual acuity of mosquitoes.

I assume if tissue samples are presented to someone who hasn't seen her they won't be effected?

Actually, I meant the opposite there; just seeing the hair/blood samples is enough to make people want to eat them.

And if someone who hasn't seen her is forced to drink her blood, they won't like it?

If they haven't visually perceived the blood before drinking it, then their reaction will be the same as if you'd forced them to drink anybody else's blood.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a new born niece. 5 months old and adorable as expected.
This gives new, horrible meaning to all the grannies who thinks she's so cute they "could just gobble her up".

Maybe 1359 was an awkward kid, but really grew into her looks that particular year. She must be very cute indeed for it to still be effective. Maybe the eye patch makes her look like she's playing pirate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have there been any animal tests? Not letting the animal attack/eat her, but putting an animal that normally wouldn't attack humans in a room with a clear-plastic wall between it and her and seeing if it tries to attack?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder if this poor woman and the old woman whose body is money are somehow related…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any organism that visually observes SCP-1359 or an accurate pictographic depiction thereof will be overcome with a single-minded determination to gain access to SCP-1359,

Does this apply to things like mosquitos?

In double-blind tests in which subjects were presented with five different hair or blood samples, one of which belonged to SCP-1359,

I assume if tissue samples are presented to someone who hasn't seen her they won't be effected? And if someone who hasn't seen her is forced to drink her blood, they won't like it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Way to turn this around! I wish everyone who wrote borderline articles could work like this. :D The final addendum about everyone eating her pictures is a great addition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let's not discuss this further.

Maybe I was reading too deeply into it, maybe not. It's a moot point now since the version I was referring to isn't the one currently in use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How does genital eating equal rape?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Basically, the most recent photographs of her look the most like how she looks now, so the effect is stronger with them.

The C-class amnestic they're dosed with afterward nullifies the craving by making them forget what SCP-1359 looks like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
80% of that 43% possessed year books from the year before SCP-1359's disappearance.

I don't get the importance of that bit.

Also, the restrained D-classes used in the containment, do they continue to crave for 1359 forever because they aren't fed parts of it, or does the effect fade eventually if they don't observe it for long?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my gooooooooooooooood

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found the main article and the tie in poem complement each other very well and the article itself is the best kind of unsettling. Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They're eating her
And now they're gonna eat me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Worst superpower ever. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Vote clearing aside, this piece adds sufficient value. The psychological implications are striking enough.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was ready to downvote this from the initial description, then changed to neutral with the doctor ambiguity, and then to an upvote when people at the photographs. It's not my favorite article, but it does enough right that I enjoyed it by the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That containment procedures. We should create a protocol for skips who need to be protected even from the SCP personnel one of these days. Anyways, I liked this a bit more (specially the part where the parents consumed her photos gave me a good impression). I can upvote and regret nothing!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. It's written believably enough, the effect is fairly interesting, and more importantly, it makes me curious how the fuck did she end up like that. Is it something that was done to her by her captor, or did he capture her because of the property?
Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The original, deleted version implied it more heavily than the rewrite, but even older incarnations of this version stated that someone had been eating parts of her genitalia. I'll concede that rape was not mentioned explicitly, but it didn't strike me as being particularly subtle.

Still, I acknowledge that Pig has toned things down since the original posting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That'll do pig. That'll do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …What rape overtones? I think yer seeing something that isn't there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, to your credit, it's a lot less in-your-face than it was originally, but the fact remains that she was locked in a basement with a diagnosed erotomaniac for six years. When you consider the fact that he didn't even start cannibalizing bits of her until half that time had elapsed, it's not hard to guess what else may have happened during that time.

Maybe I'm just oversensitive on the subject, but it still rubs me the wrong way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *edits* Have they been sufficiently removed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't see why you feel the need to include the rape overtones. The skip would be fine without it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's weird and funny. Upvoted without hesitation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it well enough, I suppose. It's not the most interesting article I've seen, but it's certainly not the worst. The only real complaint I have is that the containment procedures felt overly detailed and tedious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
BTW, the fact that you deleted the old version and posted a revised version within hours of each other bothers me. If a newer user had done that, they'd be dogpiled on for vote clearing.

You're right, I was way too hasty about it, even though the changes I did make between versions were on the biggish side. I probably should have sat on this draft longer and gotten more feedback on it first, too, since people still think it's too tasteless. I felt rushed because I was leaving the poem orphaned, but that's a shitty excuse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I specified about the passcodes because I wanted to highlight that they've decided it's necessary to put a series of three doors you need three different people to open between her and the rest of the world, not to keep her in, but to keep other people out.

EDIT: Is the current version any better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 2013 Pepper was kind of a stick in the mud. 2018 Pepper really likes the level of thought put into this, especially the ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is a woman that people want I eat

you'll have to admit it is very effective, as even just a textual description of her seems to elicit a hunger for her in readers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the old one better, and I was sort of confused why most other people didn't like it. Meh.

I still do like this one enough to upvote, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
each locked by a different passcode. Passcodes are to be changed monthly, or immediately after resolution of a containment breach. No single person is permitted to know more than one of these passcodes.

You would think that all containment chambers have passcodes, so I feel this is unnecessary.
About the skip: The poem that goes with this is really creepy, an really puts you in the place of the skip. The final addendum, however, wasn't too much of a hook: this is a woman that people want to eat. Her discovery was ok, but not too interesting. +1 for the poem, no vote for the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Completely redid the addenda from the previous version.

Also, Four Tines Blind is still tie-in poem.
